task_completion.description: Agent logs into Floreant POS using the provided Administrator PIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Floreant POS application launched and successfully bypassing the initial login screen (using PIN 1111), displaying the main dashboard or terminal.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Order Types configuration menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Order Types' configuration window or settings menu open, typically accessed via the Back Office or Configuration section of Floreant POS.

task_completion.description: Agent disables the 'HOME DELIVERY' order type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Order Types configuration window, 'HOME DELIVERY' is selected and its 'Visible', 'Enabled', or 'Show on terminal' checkbox is unchecked, or the item is otherwise marked as disabled.

task_completion.description: The 'HOME DELIVERY' option no longer appears on the main terminal screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the main order/terminal screen (where order types are selected to start a new ticket), there is NO button visible for 'HOME DELIVERY'.

task_completion.description: The 'DINE IN' and 'TAKE OUT' options remain available on the main terminal screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the main order/terminal screen, the buttons for 'DINE IN' and 'TAKE OUT' are clearly visible and available for selection.

integrity.description: Agent used the Floreant POS GUI to make changes rather than terminal commands or direct database manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and navigation within the Floreant POS application windows. No terminal windows or database management tools (e.g., command-line SQL) are used to alter the database directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic Floreant POS Java application interface, not a text editor, mock application, or web browser search.

integrity.description: Agent did not use scripts to bypass the application's configuration menus.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots showing bash scripts, Python scripts, or SQL queries being executed to update the order types.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target order type to disable is exactly 'HOME DELIVERY'. The order types 'DINE IN' and 'TAKE OUT' are default order types in Floreant POS and must remain visible on the main terminal screen after 'HOME DELIVERY' is disabled. Floreant POS uses a Java-based GUI where these order types can be configured in the Back Office settings.
